future_work_phrases: This systematic approach facilitates consistent assessments across various diseases and populations, improving the comparability of findings and supporting evidence-based decision-making
The reproducibility of this methodology allows for the integration of new data as it becomes available, maintaining the relevance and accuracy of assessments
This adaptability is particularly crucial in the context of rare diseases, where emerging information can substantially influence the understanding of disease burden and treatment efficacy

This accessible, scalable methodology enables funders and researchers, across governments, universities, healthcare organizations, and disease groups to tailor prioritization efforts according to unmet medical need
This method creates a pragmatic and transparent tool to streamline research prioritization
these outages may pose a growing threat to the cardiovascular and respiratory health of older adults
further research could better describe the lagged effects
suggesting that even short outages can have substantial population health impacts
limitation_phrases: The main limitation of this study is the combined size of the development and evaluation set being 40 diseases
making it unclear whether any true protective effect exists
The study is limited by its retrospective design
Prospective, real-time evaluation is needed before clinical implementation
outcomes for individual countries should be interpreted with caution
opening_phrases: This is a systematic methodology leveraging LLMs to quantify unmet medical needs across diverse and heterogeneous diseases
In this 2018 case-crossover study of over 23 million Fee-For-Service Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+
In this multi-stage metabolomic study involving five prospective cohorts
There is wide geographic variation in IPV-induced population immunity against poliomyelitis
In this population-based study
Despite a lack of strong evidence
We conducted a population-based case-control study
In this prospective cohort study among African women at substantial risk of HIV acquisition
transition_phrases: Several New York State-based studies have reported associations between power outage exposure and increased CVD and respiratory acute care visits
We hypothesized that power outages may lead to CVD and respiratory hospitalizations in older adults due to increased heat exposure, cold exposure, stress, and loss of electricity
Enabled by new national datasets of power outage exposure
to our knowledge, no studies have reported
The major strengths of our study include
Progress has been made in recent years

confidence in such outputs should be treated with caution
we found that the choice of model did not meaningfully influence performance for our use case during testing, though comprehensive benchmarking across models remains future work
We observed a protective effect on hospitalizations 4–5 days after a power outage, which may be a harvesting effect or an artifact
The lagged effects of CVD and respiratory hospitalizations due to power outage likely differ
We expect less exposure misclassification when ≥5% of county customers are without power compared to ≥1% of customers

ORs and 95% CIs for per SD increase in
The targeted validation stage applied quantitative profiling and measured 16 metabolites
Selection criteria of the study cohort

opening_phrases: This approach mitigates previous challenges in defining what to research, including incomplete data representation, ontological mapping difficulties, and variability in defining standard-of-care treatments
These new symptoms were more strongly associated with eclampsia than headache, visual changes or epigastric pain
Utilizing the knowledge corpus of LLMs, these challenges have been circumvented
an accessible, scalable, and maintainable assessment tool has been made
The implementation of a standardized, systematic method for generating unmet medical need scores offers benefits for the development of treatments for rare diseases
The impact of potential LLMs hallucinations were mitigated by utilizing a composite score from 11 different prompts which reduces the relative impact of any potentially incorrect individual score
An advantage of this method is the ability to reweight individual dimensions of unmet medical need, which partially mitigates the influence of preexisting health priorities
We use objective criteria and reweighting to minimize this impact
